description.en: Portugal's everyday roll, known for a deep central crease that folds it into a plump half-moon: a lean, twice-folded dough baked hot with steam for a crackling crust around a surprisingly light, airy crumb. Also a New England bakery fixture.
description.de: Portugals alltägliches Brötchen, erkennbar an der tiefen Mittelkerbe, die es zu einem prallen Halbmond faltet: ein magerer, zweifach gefalteter Teig wird heiß mit Dampf gebacken, für eine krachende Kruste um eine erstaunlich leichte, luftige Krume.
description.es: El panecillo cotidiano de Portugal, reconocible por su profunda hendidura central que lo pliega en una media luna rolliza: una masa magra, doblada dos veces, se hornea caliente con vapor para lograr una corteza crujiente sobre una miga sorprendentemente ligera.
description.fr: Le petit pain quotidien du Portugal, reconnaissable à son pli central profond qui le referme en une demi-lune bien pleine : une pâte maigre, pliée deux fois, cuite chaude à la vapeur pour une croûte craquante autour d'une mie étonnamment légère et aérée.

origin.note: The name means roughly 'dry throat'; the rolls are Portugal's most-eaten everyday bread and remain a fixture of Portuguese-American bakeries in Massachusetts and Rhode Island.

ingredients.note: for dusting the peel and proofing cloth

yield.shape: crusty half-moon roll with a deep center crease

keeping.note: Best eaten the day of baking while the crust still crackles; a daily-bakery staple in Portugal and Portuguese-American communities.

method.title: Activate yeast
method.summary: Stir yeast and sugar into lukewarm water and let it stand until foamy.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Combine flour, water and butter, rest 30 minutes, then knead in the salt and yeast mixture until the dough pulls cleanly from the bowl.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Let it double in a warm spot, about an hour.

method.title: Fold twice
method.summary: Instead of punching down, gently fold the dough over itself from all sides, rest 30 minutes, then repeat once more.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Divide and roll into tight balls, rest, flatten into discs, then press a deep crease across the center and fold into a half-moon, twisting and flattening the pointed ends to seal.

method.title: Proof
method.summary: Arrange seam-down on a floured cloth and let rise until nearly doubled.

method.title: Bake with steam
method.summary: Bake very hot with steam for the first stretch, then slightly cooler until puffed and deeply golden.

source.adaptation: Grams kept as sourced; cornmeal dusting quantity estimated since the source gave no amount; overall timing follows the source's own stated 'about 12 hours, mostly hands-off.'
